decision: Atomik distinguishes durable file authority from epistemic support and represents important claims with separate nature, evidence, verification, freshness, and workflow dimensions.
principles: Files are the durable source of record; evidence determines epistemic support.
A citation proves traceability, not automatic correctness.
Human acceptance is editorial workflow state, not factual proof.
A universal scalar truth score is not the primary trust signal.
Unsupported claims remain visible as unsupported.
Interpretive, analogical, predictive, normative, and user-synthesis content must not silently appear as direct fact.
Optional machine sidecars may support precision but cannot hide material uncertainty, dispute, or missing evidence from human-readable files and UI.
Every correction is proposed as a reviewable durable patch.
ActionTrace records execution/cost/privacy/outcome and is not epistemic evidence by itself.
Verification events and response bundles link to cross-action traces rather than owning provider-only accounting.

fields.normalized_statement: optional retrieval/deduplication form; never replaces visible statement
fields.nature: open string, known values listed in claim_natures

anchor.kind: heading | page | quote | time-range | image-region | dom | open string

fields.independence_group: optional identifier for common upstream origin

cost.status: legacy compatibility; migrate to action_trace_ids + cost_summary

cost_summary.note: ActionTrace is authoritative; this is a display/export summary.

response_bundle_extension.provider_trace: ProviderTrace[] compatibility only; provider calls become child ActionTrace records

evidence_aware_note: optional adjacent sidecar maps exact ranges/anchors

validation_rules: model-only and source-backed labels cannot both be asserted without an evidence transition record
source-backed claims require at least one inspectable EvidenceRecord
web-verified claims require a dated VerificationEvent
current-as-of claims require a date value
human-accepted cannot be converted into supported without evidence or a verification result
normative claims should expose a frame or rationale when presented as conclusions
interpretive claims should identify attribution or perspective when appropriate
contradicted/disputed claims must preserve counterevidence rather than silently deleting it
a citation anchor must resolve or be marked broken
source counts must not be treated as independence counts without independence_group analysis
